content.problemStatement:
You are given a sequence A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​. You may perform the following operation any number of times: select any two adjacent elements AiA_iAi​ and Ai+1A_{i + 1}Ai+1​ and replace one of them with Ai+Ai+12\frac{A_i + A_{i + 1}}{2}2Ai​+Ai+1​​ (a real number, i.e. without rounding). You may also select each pair of adjacent elements and each element to replace in multiple operations, i.e. any number of times.

Is it possible to make the sequence strictly increasing in a finite number of operations?
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases ≤ 10^6
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case starts with an integer N (2 ≤ N ≤ 10^5). The next line contains N space‑separated integers A1 … AN (1 ≤ Ai ≤ 10^9).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "Yes" if it is possible to make the sequence strictly increasing using the allowed operations, otherwise output "No".

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1 has no adjacent increase, so impossible. Test case 2 contains 1<2, thus possible. Test case 3 all equal, no increase. Test case 4 has several increasing adjacent pairs, so possible.

solution.approach:
The operation can only replace a chosen element by the average of it and its neighbour, which always lies between the two original values. Therefore the relative order of any adjacent pair that is non‑increasing (Ai ≥ Ai+1) can never become strictly increasing, because the new value will still be ≤ Ai and ≥ Ai+1. Consequently, the whole sequence can become strictly increasing iff there exists at least one adjacent pair with Ai < Ai+1 in the original array. The algorithm simply scans the array once and checks for such a pair.
If any Ai < Ai+1 is found, print "Yes"; otherwise print "No".
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, total O(∑N) ≤ 10^6.

code.Python3:
import sys
def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    t = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(t):
        n = int(next(it))
        prev = int(next(it))
        ok = False
        for _ in range(1, n):
            cur = int(next(it))
            if prev < cur:
                ok = True
            prev = cur
        out.append('Yes' if ok else 'No')
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))
if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
